basics.name: Miguel Madrid-Mencía
basics.label: Senior Backend & Cloud Engineer | Bioinformatics & AI Systems
basics.summary: Senior Backend and Cloud Engineer with 10+ years across public/private sectors in research, genomics, and software consulting. Designing secure, reproducible production systems for cloud-native services, CI/CD, Machine Learning and Generative AI. Co-Author of 7 peer-reviewed scientific publications (100+ citations) and co-author of a patent in progress with Illumina.

work.summary: Applied ML Systems. Technology services to drive action and impact through cloud, data, AI, and platforms.
highlights: Built Voice AI testing framework for production systems with a focus on validation, traceability, and operational reliability.
Led improvements in CI/CD pipelines, code quality, and security standards for a US financial client.

work.summary: Acquired by Egen.ai in 2024. Applied ML Systems.
highlights: Built and deployed cloud-native GCP services for healthcare and precision medicine, optimizing scalability, reliability, and cost.
Implemented Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD workflows, reducing deployment risk and operational complexity.
Contributed to internal best practices around software quality, reproducibility, and DevSecOps.

work.summary: Genetic analysis technologies: personalized medicine, disease research, drug development.
highlights: Benchmarked privacy-preserving data solutions from partners, evaluating performance and regulatory compatibility.
Designed machine learning pipelines and reproducible workflows across HPC, AWS, and Illumina Connected Analytics (ICA).
Led automation initiatives improving reliability and user experience in large-scale genomics platforms.
Designed and optimized Snowflake-based data models for genomics and GWAS pipelines.
Co-authored a patent in progress in applied bioinformatics and genomic data processing.

work.summary: Stimulate innovation in terms of research and teaching in the fight against cancer.
highlights: Designed reproducible ML pipelines for multi-omics cancer research.
Contributed to 7 international peer-reviewed publications in oncology and systems biology.
Developed interactive web tools and microservices to make complex bioinformatics workflows accessible to researchers.
Managed IT infrastructure and DevOps practices for a multidisciplinary bioinformatics team.

work.summary: High performance computing (HPC) MareNostrum, a leading European supercomputer.
highlights: Developed a platform for AQuAS to analyze health outcomes and quality indicators through an interactive geospatial map.
Improved testing and workflow reproducibility for the Elixir / OpenEBench European HPC infrastructure.

work.summary: Develop new and more effective approaches to preventing, diagnosing and treating cancer.
highlights: Developed a reproducible workflow to identify mutation clusters in protein structures, including web-based visualization.
Contributed to the European OpenMinTeD NLP/text-mining project, developing services and APIs for biomedical text analysis.

education.area: Master in Bioinformatics and Computational Biology
education.summary: Master's Thesis: Structure-PPi v2.0: Module for Annotating Cancer-Related Single-Nucleotide Variants at Protein-Protein Interfaces.
Bachelor's Thesis: Computer Vision Adapted to Optical Coherence Tomographies.
